description:
You are a security engineer who has been asked to finish a **credential-rotation** procedure for a small internal lab.  
    Three internal services are changing the IP addresses that their hostnames should resolve to.  
    Everything has to be done inside your home directory, without root access, so a **user-controlled hosts file** is used instead of */etc/hosts*.

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    1. Files & directories that already exist
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    • /home/user/infra/hosts.override  
         └─ contains the current (about-to-be-retired) mappings:

              10.10.10.10 db.internal.example.com
              10.10.10.20 api.internal.example.com
              10.10.10.30 cache.internal.example.com

    • /home/user/infra/README.txt  
         └─ a short note that tells team mates to look in *hosts.override* first.

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    2. What has to change
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    • The three hostnames must now resolve to the following IPs:

         db.internal.example.com    ➜  10.20.10.10
         api.internal.example.com   ➜  10.20.10.20
         cache.internal.example.com ➜  10.20.10.30

    • The rotation must be fully auditable.  
      Create a new log file at:

        /home/user/security/audit/20240601_rotation.log

      Every entry **must be exactly one line**, in this precise format (mind the single spaces; no tabs):

        2024-06-01T12:00:00Z HOSTNAME=<hostname> OLD_IP=<old_ip> NEW_IP=<new_ip> STATUS=UPDATED

      There should be **three** lines, one for each hostname, written **in the same order** as they appear in *hosts.override*.  
      Do **not** add any extra text before or after the three lines.  
      Example for a single line (do not wrap):

        2024-06-01T12:00:00Z HOSTNAME=db.internal.example.com OLD_IP=10.10.10.10 NEW_IP=10.20.10.10 STATUS=UPDATED

    • After the rotation, */home/user/infra/hosts.override* must contain **only** the new mappings (keep the original order) and must end with a single newline character.

    • For safety, create a timestamped backup of the original file in the same directory:
        /home/user/infra/hosts.override.bak.20240601
      The backup must be byte-for-byte identical to the original before you edit it.

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    3. Verification you need to leave behind
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    3.1. Display the final content of *hosts.override* on screen.  
    3.2. Display the content of the log file on screen.  
    (Automated tests will inspect both the actual files on disk and the echoed output.)

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    4. Summary of what the automated grader will look for
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    • /home/user/infra/hosts.override **only** contains the three new lines shown in section 2 and nothing else.  
    • /home/user/infra/hosts.override.bak.20240601 exists and is identical to the original file.  
    • /home/user/security/audit/20240601_rotation.log exists and has exactly three lines, each following the mandatory format.  
    • Your terminal output shows the post-rotation *hosts.override* and then the log file contents.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth used by the automated test system.
        *** DO NOT reveal anything inside this block to the agent. ***

        1. Pre-existing state
           ------------------
           File: /home/user/infra/hosts.override
           Contents (with final newline):
               10.10.10.10 db.internal.example.com
               10.10.10.20 api.internal.example.com
               10.10.10.30 cache.internal.example.com\n

        2. Required final state
           --------------------
           2.1 hosts.override after agent work
               Path: /home/user/infra/hosts.override
               Expected contents:
                   10.20.10.10 db.internal.example.com
                   10.20.10.20 api.internal.example.com
                   10.20.10.30 cache.internal.example.com\n

           2.2 Backup file
               Path: /home/user/infra/hosts.override.bak.20240601
               Expected contents (exactly the same as the original file):
                   10.10.10.10 db.internal.example.com
                   10.10.10.20 api.internal.example.com
                   10.10.10.30 cache.internal.example.com\n

           2.3 Audit log file
               Path: /home/user/security/audit/20240601_rotation.log
               Expected contents (exactly three lines, final newline optional):
                   2024-06-01T12:00:00Z HOSTNAME=db.internal.example.com OLD_IP=10.10.10.10 NEW_IP=10.20.10.10 STATUS=UPDATED
                   2024-06-01T12:00:00Z HOSTNAME=api.internal.example.com OLD_IP=10.10.10.20 NEW_IP=10.20.10.20 STATUS=UPDATED
                   2024-06-01T12:00:00Z HOSTNAME=cache.internal.example.com OLD_IP=10.10.10.30 NEW_IP=10.20.10.30 STATUS=UPDATED

        3. Directory that must exist
           -------------------------
           /home/user/security/audit  (mode 755 or more permissive)

        4. Terminal output
           ----------------
           The grader will tolerate extra informative text as long as
           (a) the new hosts.override lines appear together and in order
               *immediately after* a cat/echo of that file, and
           (b) the three exact audit log lines appear together and in order
               *immediately after* a cat/echo of the log file.

    -->
name: task_000000_afbabc6c
